title: Batatas purga
taxonomy: Batatas purga is a genus of flowering plants in the dayflower family, Convolvulaceae. The genus Batatas is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa, Asia, and Australia. It consists of about 40 species, some of which are used as food, particularly in Africa. The genus is related to the genus Ipomoea, which includes sweet potatoes and morning glories. The species within the Batatas genus are characterized by their edible tubers and heart-shaped leaves.
